message:
Add /tweak skill to open editor at specific line

Opens the user's GUI editor at the exact line of the last edit.
Detects running editor (Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Zed, etc.)
via lsappinfo on macOS.

🔮 View transcript: https://agentlogs.ai/s/u8c3q0nps62oij24racaaeop

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+name: tweak
+description: Open the user's default editor at a specific line to manually tweak styles or code. Use when the user wants to make manual adjustments after you've made changes.
+---
+
+# Tweak
+
+Opens the user's default editor at a specific file and line number, allowing them to manually tweak the code.
+
+## Usage
+
+After making changes to a file, if the user invokes `/tweak`, run the `open-editor.sh` script from this skill directory:
+
+```bash
+~/.claude/skills/tweak/open-editor.sh <file_path> <start_line> [end_line]
+```
+
+## Instructions
+
+1. When the user says `/tweak`, identify the **last file and line range you edited** in the conversation
+2. Run the script with the file path, start line, and end line (for multi-line edits)
+3. The script auto-detects which GUI editor is running (Cursor, VS Code, Zed, etc.) and opens the file with the range selected
+
+## Example
+
+Single line edit at line 42:
+```bash
+~/.claude/skills/tweak/open-editor.sh /Users/philipp/dev/app/src/Button.tsx 42
+```
+
+Multi-line edit from line 42 to 58 (opens at start line; range selection only works in Zed):
+```bash
+~/.claude/skills/tweak/open-editor.sh /Users/philipp/dev/app/src/Button.tsx 42 58
+```

@@ -0,0 +1,133 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+
+# Opens a file at a specific line (with optional selection range) in a GUI editor
+# Usage: open-editor.sh <file_path> <start_line> [end_line]
+
+FILE="$1"
+START_LINE="${2:-1}"
+END_LINE="${3:-}"
+
+if [ -z "$FILE" ]; then
+  echo "Usage: open-editor.sh <file_path> <start_line> [end_line]"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+if [ ! -f "$FILE" ]; then
+  echo "File not found: $FILE"
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+# VS Code forks (all use same -g syntax)
+VSCODE_FORKS=(cursor windsurf code codium positron antigravity void melty)
+
+# GUI editors in preference order
+GUI_EDITORS=("${VSCODE_FORKS[@]}" zed subl idea webstorm)
+
+# Detect which GUI editor is currently running (most recently used first via lsappinfo)
+detect_running_editor() {
+  # On macOS, check running GUI apps
+  if [[ "$OSTYPE" == darwin* ]]; then
+    local running_apps
+    running_apps=$(lsappinfo list | grep -Ei 'Cursor|Windsurf|Visual Studio Code|VSCodium|Positron|Antigravity|Void|Melty|Zed|Sublime|IntelliJ|WebStorm|PyCharm' | head -10)
+
+    # Check VS Code forks first
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Cursor"; then
+      command -v cursor &>/dev/null && echo "cursor" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Windsurf"; then
+      command -v windsurf &>/dev/null && echo "windsurf" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Visual Studio Code"; then
+      command -v code &>/dev/null && echo "code" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "VSCodium"; then
+      command -v codium &>/dev/null && echo "codium" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Positron"; then
+      command -v positron &>/dev/null && echo "positron" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Antigravity"; then
+      command -v antigravity &>/dev/null && echo "antigravity" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Void"; then
+      command -v void &>/dev/null && echo "void" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Melty"; then
+      command -v melty &>/dev/null && echo "melty" && return
+    fi
+    # Other editors
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Zed"; then
+      command -v zed &>/dev/null && echo "zed" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "Sublime"; then
+      command -v subl &>/dev/null && echo "subl" && return
+    fi
+    if echo "$running_apps" | grep -qi "IntelliJ\|WebStorm\|PyCharm"; then
+      for ide in idea webstorm pycharm; do
+        command -v "$ide" &>/dev/null && echo "$ide" && return
+      done
+    fi
+  fi
+
+  # Fallback: check what's installed
+  for editor in "${GUI_EDITORS[@]}"; do
+    if command -v "$editor" &>/dev/null; then
+      echo "$editor"
+      return
+    fi
+  done
+
+  echo ""
+}
+
+EDITOR_CMD=$(detect_running_editor)
+
+if [ -z "$EDITOR_CMD" ]; then
+  echo "No GUI editor found. Please install Cursor, VS Code, Windsurf, Zed, or Sublime Text."
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+EDITOR_NAME=$(basename "$EDITOR_CMD")
+
+# Check if it's a VS Code fork
+is_vscode_fork() {
+  local name="$1"
+  for fork in "${VSCODE_FORKS[@]}"; do
+    [[ "$name" == "$fork" ]] && return 0
+  done
+  return 1
+}
+
+if is_vscode_fork "$EDITOR_NAME"; then
+  # All VS Code forks: -g file:line:column (no range selection via CLI)
+  "$EDITOR_CMD" -r -g "$FILE:$START_LINE:1"
+else
+  case "$EDITOR_NAME" in
+    zed)
+      # Zed: file:startLine:startCol,endLine:endCol
+      if [ -n "$END_LINE" ]; then
+        END_COL=$(sed -n "${END_LINE}p" "$FILE" | wc -c | tr -d ' ')
+        "$EDITOR_CMD" "$FILE:$START_LINE:1,$END_LINE:$END_COL"
+      else
+        "$EDITOR_CMD" "$FILE:$START_LINE"
+      fi
+      ;;
+    subl)
+      # Sublime Text: file:line
+      "$EDITOR_CMD" "$FILE:$START_LINE"
+      ;;
+    idea|webstorm|pycharm|goland|rubymine|phpstorm)
+      # JetBrains IDEs: --line line file
+      "$EDITOR_CMD" --line "$START_LINE" "$FILE"
+      ;;
+    *)
+      "$EDITOR_CMD" "$FILE:$START_LINE"
+      ;;
+  esac
+fi
+
+if [ -n "$END_LINE" ]; then
+  echo "Opened $FILE:$START_LINE-$END_LINE in $EDITOR_NAME"
+else
+  echo "Opened $FILE:$START_LINE in $EDITOR_NAME"
+fi
